name: Mirador de la Lastrilla y población de Ucero — circular por el Parque natural Cañón del río Lobos
region_intro: Road cycling around San Leonardo De Yagüe offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by the nearby Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. The region features a mix of forested areas, river valleys, and open landscapes, providing diverse cycling experiences. Elevation changes are common, with routes navigating hills and offering panoramic views. This area is suitable for road cyclists seeking both moderate and challenging rides. Best road cycling routes around San Leonardo De Yagüe The most popular road cycling route is Cañón del Río Lobos and Hontoria del Pinar Loop, a 34.5 miles (55.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the natural park and connects to Hontoria del Pinar. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mirador de la Lastrilla y población de Ucero — circular por el Parque natural Cañón del río Lobos, a moderate 25.9 miles (41.7 km) path. This route offers views from Mirador de la Lastrilla and circles through the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. Local road cyclists also love the San Leonardo de Yagüe – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail leading through the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around San Leonardo De Yagüe is defined by river canyons, natural parks, and forested hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Leonardo De Yagüe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a handful of reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Leonardo De Yagüe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: The region offers varied terrain, characterized by the nearby Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. You'll find a mix of forested areas, river valleys, and open landscapes. Elevation changes are common, with routes navigating hills and providing panoramic views, suitable for both moderate and challenging rides.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: Yes, San Leonardo De Yagüe has routes suitable for beginners. Out of the 14 road cycling routes available, at least one is classified as easy, offering a gentler introduction to the area's beautiful scenery.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151125" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castroviejo Viewpoint – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from San Leonardo de Yagüe&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 87.2 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options around San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: While many routes involve elevation, there are options that can be enjoyed by families, particularly those with some cycling experience. Look for routes classified as 'easy' or 'moderate' that stick to flatter sections or well-maintained paths, allowing for a more relaxed pace.

Q: What are some scenic highlights I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Grande (Río Lobos Canyon)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5647575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Seven Eyes&lt;/a&gt; is another picturesque spot.

Q: Are there many loop road cycling trails in San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in San Leonardo De Yagüe are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070955277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón del Río Lobos and Hontoria del Pinar Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31859084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Leonardo de Yagüe – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: The road cycling routes in San Leonardo De Yagüe are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from river canyons to forested hills, and the well-curated routes that explore the natural park.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through villages or offer cafe stops?
A: Many routes in the region connect to or pass through small towns and villages, such as Hontoria del Pinar or Ucero. These locations often provide opportunities for cafe stops, allowing you to refuel and experience local hospitality. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256356845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Lastrilla y población de Ucero — circular por el Parque natural Cañón del río Lobos&lt;/a&gt; passes through Ucero.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: There are 14 distinct road cycling routes available around San Leonardo De Yagüe, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in the area?
A: While the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park is a popular draw, exploring routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de La Blanca – San Leonardo de Yagüe loop from San Leonardo de Yagüe&lt;/a&gt; can lead you to less-trafficked roads and serene landscapes, offering a sense of discovery away from the main tourist paths.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Leonardo De Yagüe?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in San Leonardo De Yagüe, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, especially in the midday sun, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

In the old middle of the Río Lobos Natural Park is this spectacular space where the Río Loboso (which is born from the waters of the slopes of these mountains) flows. From this point you can head towards the Hermitage of San Bartolomé, where much of it is paved.
